steps.title: Identify the range
steps.content:
The normal body temperature for an adult is given as between 97.8 degrees Fahrenheit and 99 degrees Fahrenheit, inclusive. This can be written as a compound inequality:
$$97.8 \leq T \leq 99$$.
Where \(T\) represents Kevin's body temperature.

steps.title: Evaluate each option
steps.content:
• Choice A: 97.7°F - This falls outside the lower range.
  • Choice B: 97.6°F - This also falls below the required range.
  • Choice C: 97.9°F - This satisfies the inequality as it is within the acceptable range.
  • Choice D: 99.7°F - This is above the upper limit of 99 degrees Fahrenheit and thus outside the normal range.

Only choice C fits within the specified temperature parameters — C is correct.
